page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon cores running at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, and AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. The instance achieved an average rating in stress-ng single-core CPU performance with a score of 1404.38 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1135.0. However, multi-core execution is relatively limited, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2492.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3680.85. Memory bandwidth tests show 26.28 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 22.5 GB/sec for write operations. Database and caching operations, such as Redis SET, reach 3.61 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is measured at 3.87 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the n2-highmem-4 offers a cost-effective profile for memory-bound applications that do not demand high parallel processing power. By pairing a generous 32.0 GB memory footprint with only 4 vCPUs, it avoids the licensing and resource costs associated with larger multi-core instances. This makes it highly suitable for workloads such as moderate in-memory caching, small-scale databases, enterprise applications with high memory overhead, and lightweight data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU acceleration. Single-core performance is moderate, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 1135.0, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are more constrained, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2492.0 and uncached read bandwidth of 26.28 GB/sec. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications that do not require extensive parallel compute. It is best suited for in-memory caching, small databases, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-4 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage or GPU acceleration.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive applications without the need for high core counts.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Support for AVX-512 vector instructions and AES cryptography
Moderate single-core performance with constrained multi-core scaling
Best suited for in-memory caching, small databases, and memory-heavy workloads
